question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Short answer / classification - This matches the pattern from ticket TI-931293: after Windows updates and a VM restore, SAP printing stopped while normal follow‑me printing continued. The most likely cause is broken/changed network‑share or queue publishing settings on the SAP print servers (queue-level sharing/publishing), not the spooler itself. In TI-931293 the issue was resolved by re‑disabling and re‑enabling the network share for all affected printer queues rather than rolling back Windows

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . Why this pattern points to queue/share issues (not spooler) - Symptoms you describe (SAP jobs failing while normal Office/follow‑me printing works; spooler restart and server reboot did not help; recent Windows update + VM restore) are textbook for a corrupted or changed queue publishing / network‑share state that breaks SAP’s print paths. The SAP printserver standard explicitly calls out queue properties (publishing/sharing) and post‑change validation after updates/restores as first checkpoints
context: SOURCE 7: ﻿ general info & description Print Services for SAP Output Standard Version 1.0 Date: 25.07.2024 Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the provisioning of SAP outputs (e.g., delivery notes, pick lists, labels) via central Windows print servers at Customer Company 13. The objective is a stable operation of SAP output processes, as printing disruptions can have direct impact on logistics and deliveries. The standard covers the technical provisioning of SAP-relevant print queues on dedicated print servers as well as the operational guidelines following Windows updates, restores, or maintenance windows. Special focus is placed on the consistency of printer queue configuration (Queue Properties) and network sharing and publishing mechanisms, as these may be relevant for SAP print paths and spool processing. Typical symptoms of deviations from this standard include: print jobs remaining stuck in SAP, print jobs disappearing from the queue without printing, or print jobs accumulating on the print server and only being released after manual intervention. This pattern was observed in ticket TI-931293 after a Windows update and subsequent VM restore. design Architecture overview SAP System → SAP Spool / Output Management → SAP print target (logical printer definition) → Windows Printserver (SAP Printserver) → Print queues → Physical printers Roles and components 1. SAP Output Management Generates print jobs and transfers them to defined print targets. The availability and correct processing of print targets is business-critical. 2. SAP Printserver (Windows) Serves as the central processing instance for SAP printing. The relevant queues are operated on dedicated print servers (in the ticket: VRINFRGERPRNT03 and VRINFRGERPRNT04). 3. Print queues Queue properties must be standardized and consistent. After system changes (updates, restores), it must be ensured that publishing/sharing parameters and queue status are correct and that no unintended states (e.g., stuck jobs) exist. 4. Print Spooler Service The service is responsible for processing print jobs. A restart may temporarily influence symptoms but does not necessarily resolve configuration deviations at queue level. Operational principles • After Windows updates or VM restores, a validation of the SAP print servers must be performed (queue status, spooler, sharing/publishing of queues, test print from SAP). • SAP print paths must be treated as business-critical; disruptions are to be handled with priority (classified as PRIO HIGH in the ticket). • If printing issues occur after update/restore, queue configuration must be treated as the primary checkpoint, not only the spooler service. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue affect only printing from SAP or also normal printing (e.g., Office)? A) Only SAP printing affected, normal printing works A1: Are all SAP print targets affected or only specific print servers? • Only specific print servers affected → Perform SAP printserver-specific checks (queue status, publishing/sharing, spooler, restore state) • All print servers affected → Check SAP output configuration (print targets, spool queue, SAP-side errors) A2: Was there a Windows update or VM restore within the last 24–72 hours? • Yes → Perform post-change validation according to standard (queue properties, sharing/publishing, test print) • No → Proceed to A3 A3: Are SAP print jobs visible on the print server (queue) or do they remain stuck in SAP? • Remain stuck in SAP → Check SAP output / connection to print target • Visible on the print server but no print output → Check queue/spooler/sharing mechanism B) SAP printing and normal printing affected B1: Is the Print Spooler service running? • No → Start service / check service recovery • Yes → Proceed to B2 B2: Are queues paused or are jobs permanently stuck? • Yes → Correct queue status, clear stuck jobs, then perform test print • No → Check network path / printer reachability C) Issue occurs after update/restore and is printserver-specific C1: Check queue properties according to standard • Validate publishing/sharing status of printer queues • If inconsistent state: disable/enable relevant queue parameters as a reset measure This procedure corresponds to the pattern documented in the ticket, where after update and restore the network sharing/queue functionality had to be corrected. context: SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules. context: SOURCE 7: ﻿ general info & description Print Services for SAP Output Standard Version 1.0 Date: 25.07.2024 Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the provisioning of SAP outputs (e.g., delivery notes, pick lists, labels) via central Windows print servers at Customer Company 13. The objective is a stable operation of SAP output processes, as printing disruptions can have direct impact on logistics and deliveries. The standard covers the technical provisioning of SAP-relevant print queues on dedicated print servers as well as the operational guidelines following Windows updates, restores, or maintenance windows. Special focus is placed on the consistency of printer queue configuration (Queue Properties) and network sharing and publishing mechanisms, as these may be relevant for SAP print paths and spool processing. Typical symptoms of deviations from this standard include: print jobs remaining stuck in SAP, print jobs disappearing from the queue without printing, or print jobs accumulating on the print server and only being released after manual intervention. This pattern was observed in ticket TI-931293 after a Windows update and subsequent VM restore. design Architecture overview SAP System → SAP Spool / Output Management → SAP print target (logical printer definition) → Windows Printserver (SAP Printserver) → Print queues → Physical printers Roles and components 1. SAP Output Management Generates print jobs and transfers them to defined print targets. The availability and correct processing of print targets is business-critical. 2. SAP Printserver (Windows) Serves as the central processing instance for SAP printing. The relevant queues are operated on dedicated print servers (in the ticket: VRINFRGERPRNT03 and VRINFRGERPRNT04). 3. Print queues Queue properties must be standardized and consistent. After system changes (updates, restores), it must be ensured that publishing/sharing parameters and queue status are correct and that no unintended states (e.g., stuck jobs) exist. 4. Print Spooler Service The service is responsible for processing print jobs. A restart may temporarily influence symptoms but does not necessarily resolve configuration deviations at queue level. Operational principles • After Windows updates or VM restores, a validation of the SAP print servers must be performed (queue status, spooler, sharing/publishing of queues, test print from SAP). • SAP print paths must be treated as business-critical; disruptions are to be handled with priority (classified as PRIO HIGH in the ticket). • If printing issues occur after update/restore, queue configuration must be treated as the primary checkpoint, not only the spooler service. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue affect only printing from SAP or also normal printing (e.g., Office)? A) Only SAP printing affected, normal printing works A1: Are all SAP print targets affected or only specific print servers? • Only specific print servers affected → Perform SAP printserver-specific checks (queue status, publishing/sharing, spooler, restore state) • All print servers affected → Check SAP output configuration (print targets, spool queue, SAP-side errors) A2: Was there a Windows update or VM restore within the last 24–72 hours? • Yes → Perform post-change validation according to standard (queue properties, sharing/publishing, test print) • No → Proceed to A3 A3: Are SAP print jobs visible on the print server (queue) or do they remain stuck in SAP? • Remain stuck in SAP → Check SAP output / connection to print target • Visible on the print server but no print output → Check queue/spooler/sharing mechanism B) SAP printing and normal printing affected B1: Is the Print Spooler service running? • No → Start service / check service recovery • Yes → Proceed to B2 B2: Are queues paused or are jobs permanently stuck? • Yes → Correct queue status, clear stuck jobs, then perform test print • No → Check network path / printer reachability C) Issue occurs after update/restore and is printserver-specific C1: Check queue properties according to standard • Validate publishing/sharing status of printer queues • If inconsistent state: disable/enable relevant queue parameters as a reset measure This procedure corresponds to the pattern documented in the ticket, where after update and restore the network sharing/queue functionality had to be corrected.

context: SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules. context: SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
